content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

February 8, 2002

Larry Walker Auditor Committee
Post Office Box 968
Chino, CA 91708-0968

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-01-302

Dear Mr. Walker:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

How should two candidates report receipts from a joint fundraiser?

CONCLUSION

See analysis below.

FACTS

The Larry Walker (Walker) and Herb Fischer (Fischer) committees held a joint fundraising reception in November. Contributions were made payable to Fischer, as directed by the invitation. Expenses were paid ½ each by the two committees, with each committee writing a check for ½ directly to the vendor. The committees agreed to divide the proceeds of the event 50% each. Fischer now has (approximately) $34,000, and will write a Fischer Committee check for $17,000 to Walker to accomplish the agreed-upon division.

_______________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-01-302
Page No. 2

ANALYSIS

Candidate Fischer's Report:

Schedule A: Mr. Fischer must report $17,000 as contributions received on
Schedule A, itemized as to each proportional amount to be allocated to that campaign.
For example, a $1,000 check from XYZ, Inc. is to be reported as a $500 contribution
received by Fischer. The resulting Schedule A would show a total of $17,000 in
contributions received.

Schedule I: To account for the other $17,000 Fischer received (and which will be
forwarded to Walker), Fischer should show a positive adjustment to cash of $17,000,
with the explanation that this amount was received on Walker's behalf, and has been
forwarded to Walker.

Walker Report

Schedule A: Mr. Walker should report the $17,000 received from Fischer by
listing essentially the same information that Fischer listed on Schedule A. For example,
the XYZ, Inc. check will be shown as a $500 contribution here also. Fischer will be cited
as an intermediary for the entire $17,000.

A small number of checks were also received by Walker (all under $100, and
totaling under $1,000). You should handle these in the same way as above, with roles
reversed, and note that no additional itemization of contributors will result, but an
additional amount for contributions under $100 will result on both reports. Also, a much
smaller adjustment to cash will appear on the Walker report.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menehaca
General Counsel

By: C. Scott Tocher
Counsel, Legal Division

CST:jg
